response:
# Diagnosis or brief
Mode: diagnose. Domain: IS. Model supplied: yes. Verdict: ineligible. The proposed story routes posts using true labels that arrive only after moderation, whereas the model chooses one score threshold ex ante from historical conditional score distributions.

# Proposed narrative
A platform fixes one moderation score threshold using historical conditional score distributions and error weights before future posts and labels realize. Each future score is classified by that threshold. Because the score distributions overlap, the rule trades the two weighted classification errors and necessarily leaves residual error; it neither observes future labels nor guarantees elimination of harmful content.

# Decision structure (DFC-12)
Actor: platform moderation-policy owner. Trigger: setting a future-post moderation rule from historical distributions. Choices: one score threshold. Decision-time information: historical conditional score distributions and error weights; future scores and true labels are unknown. Stakes: expected weighted classification error. Frictions: threshold fixed before future labels and only one cutoff is allowed. Mechanism: the threshold partitions realized scores, with overlap producing errors. Trade-off: reducing one classification error increases the other. Counterfactual: alternative feasible ex ante thresholds. Model mapping summary: threshold is decision; future label and score are random variables; classification is outcome; expected weighted error is objective. Evidence status summary: harm elimination is unsupported. Boundaries summary: no clairvoyant routing, perfect classification, or total-harm guarantee.

# Consistency gates
Actor—pass: the platform controls the threshold. Timing—fail: the proposed story uses labels that arrive after moderation. Information—fail: future true labels are treated as decision-time inputs. Behavior—fail: clairvoyant routing replaces score-threshold classification with residual error. Constraints—fail: per-post future-label routing violates the single ex ante threshold policy. Objective—pass: the repaired framing retains expected weighted classification error. Eligible: false.

# Model mapping
one score threshold | decision | common moderation cutoff | fixed before future labels | cannot route on later labels
historical conditional score distributions | observation | estimated score behavior by class | available ex ante | basis for expected error, not perfect foresight
future true label | random_variable | later-established content class | unavailable at moderation | cannot be a routing input
score realization | random_variable | future classifier score | realized for classification | noisy because distributions overlap
classification outcome | outcome | threshold-based moderation result | produced from score | retains false positives and negatives
expected weighted classification error | objective | weighted future error | minimized in expectation | not total harm eliminated

# Claims
The rule eliminates harmful content — unsupported_claim; source: null; remove and report residual error. Future labels are available at moderation — unsupported_claim; source: null; remove. Historical conditional distributions generalize to future posts — model_assumption; source: null; state and test for drift.

# Boundaries
The model supports only ex ante expected error comparisons under historical distributions. It does not provide clairvoyant labels, perfect routing, causal harm elimination, or robustness to distribution shift.

# Most important remaining weakness
Future validity of the historical score distributions is assumed and residual classification error remains.

# Mode result
Verdict: ineligible. Failed gates: timing, information, behavior, constraints. Repairs: choose and fix one score threshold using historical distributions, apply it to future scores, and report both residual error types. Routing on future true labels would require a different information structure and model.
